title: Finding Hidden Treasure in Fool's Gold
summary: Scientists have made an exciting discovery about a shiny mineral called pyrite, often called fool's gold. They found that pyrite, which is found in rocks like shale, contains a special metal called lithium. Lithium is very important because it is used in batteries for phones, electric cars, and to store energy from the sun and wind. As we need more clean energy, finding new sources of lithium is a big challenge. Usually, lithium is mined from rocks, but scientists are now looking in unexpected places. A team from West Virginia University studied old shale rocks and found lithium inside pyrite, which was a surprise because no one thought these two could be together. This could mean we have a new way to get lithium from rocks that are common, but more research is needed to see if this works in other places too.

background_read: Lithium is a special metal that helps store energy in batteries, which we use every day in things like phones and electric cars. As the world tries to use more clean energy from the sun and wind, we need more lithium. Usually, we get lithium by mining it from rocks, but this can be hard and not always good for the environment. Scientists are always looking for new places to find lithium, like in old rocks or waste materials, to make sure we have enough for the future.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from West Virginia University
WHAT: Found lithium inside pyrite in shale rocks
WHERE: In the Appalachian basin in the United States
WHY: To find new sources of lithium for batteries and clean energy

perspectives.description: Finding lithium in pyrite is a new and unexpected way to get this important metal.
